# Xaloqi EDS

> Production-grade ISO 14229 UDS + ISO 15765-2 ISO-TP + ISO 13400-2 DoIP diagnostics stack
> for Zephyr RTOS and FreeRTOS. YAML-driven code generation produces ASIL-B safety-wrapped
> C firmware. v1.6.0.

## What EDS Does

EDS is a commercially licensable automotive diagnostics platform implementing ISO 14229 (UDS),
ISO 15765-2 (ISO-TP), and ISO 13400-2 (DoIP) for Zephyr RTOS and FreeRTOS. A developer
describes their ECU's diagnostic interface in a YAML file, runs the code generator, and
receives complete ASIL-B-safety-wrapped C source files, a pytest test suite, and CANoe CAPL
scripts ready to use immediately. Transport — CAN/ISO-TP or Ethernet/DoIP — is a one-field
YAML choice. The same UDS core, safety chain, and codegen pipeline serve both transports.

EDS implements:
- ISO 14229 (UDS): 16 service handlers (0x10 0x11 0x14 0x19 0x22 0x27 0x28 0x2E 0x2F 0x31 0x34 0x35 0x36 0x37 0x3E 0x85)
- ISO 15765-2 (ISO-TP): SF/FF/CF/FC, N_As/N_Bs/N_Cr timers, STmin, Flow Control
- ISO 13400-2 (DoIP): ECU server — Routing Activation, DiagnosticMessage dispatch, Positive/Negative Ack, Alive Check; Zephyr (zsock_*) and FreeRTOS+LwIP bindings (v1.6.0)
- ASIL-B safety wrapper chain: 5-step mandatory validation on every DID access
- AES-128-CMAC SecurityAccess (0x27) with TRNG entropy requirement
- YAML-driven codegen: diagnostics_config.yaml → 14 generated C/H files + test suite + optional OpenSOVD 1.0 CDA JSON (--sovd)
- Auto-generated pytest test suites (per-DID, per-routine, service protocol, firmware harness)
- Auto-generated CANoe CAPL test scripts
- 12 reference ECU examples: basic_ecu, basic_ecu_doip, basic_ecu_freertos, basic_ecu_doip_freertos, bms_ecu, motor_controller_ecu, ardep_ecu, sensor_ecu, sensor_ecu_freertos, safeboot_ecu, safeboot_freertos_ecu, robot_joint_controller_ecu
- MCP server: exposes generate_did_config, run_codegen, validate_asil_b, explain_uds_error
- React/TypeScript live dashboard GUI
- VS Code extension (YAML validation, hover docs, one-click codegen)
- 8-job CI pipeline: unit tests (37 modules) · integration · static analysis · Zephyr builds · FreeRTOS QEMU ARM (basic + OTA safeboot) · DoIP integration (native_sim + DoipBus) · harness tests (68) · SOVD CDA generation

## Licensing

Community tier: GPL v2 (runtime stack: core/, transport/, config/, platform/)
ECU examples: Apache 2.0 (examples/)
Developer tier: Commercial €690/yr (codegen + testgen + templates + VS Code ext + integration guide)
Professional tier: Commercial €1,990/yr (Developer + Safety Manual + MISRA log + RTM + priority support)

## Quick Integration (Zephyr, CAN/ISO-TP)

1. Write your_ecu/diagnostics_config.yaml describing DIDs, DTCs, and routines
2. Run: python3 tools/codegen.py --config your_ecu/diagnostics_config.yaml \
         --out your_ecu/generated/ --safety-wrappers --asil-level B --test-gen
3. Implement DID handler functions declared in your_ecu/generated/did_handlers.h
4. Wire EDS sources into your CMakeLists.txt (see docs/INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md Step 3)
5. Build: west build -b native_sim examples/basic_ecu

## Quick Integration (Zephyr, DoIP/Ethernet)

1. Add ecu.transport: doip and ecu.doip block to diagnostics_config.yaml
2. Run codegen (identical command — same YAML schema, same templates)
3. Add -DEDS_DOIP_ONLY_BUILD to CMakeLists.txt; add doip_server.c + zephyr_lwip.c + platform_doip.c to target_sources
4. Call uds_generated_init(NULL, 0U, 0U) then eds_doip_platform_start(logical_addr, DOIP_PORT, srv)
5. See docs/INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md Section 5b for the complete DoIP walkthrough

## Quick Integration (FreeRTOS)

1. Write your_ecu/diagnostics_config.yaml (same format as Zephyr)
2. Run codegen (identical command — same YAML, same templates)
3. Implement eds_platform_init() callbacks: can_send, optional NVM ops
4. Call eds_platform_init() → uds_generated_init() → eds_freertos_start() → vTaskStartScheduler()
5. See docs/INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md section 4 for full FreeRTOS walkthrough

## YAML Config Schema

Minimum viable config (CAN/ISO-TP):

```yaml
schema_version: 1
metadata:
  ecu_name: "MyECU"
  version:  "1.0.0"

timing:
  p2_server_max_ms:       25
  p2_star_server_max_ms:  5000
  s3_server_timeout_ms:   5000

dids:
  - id:   "0xF190"
    name: "VehicleIdentificationNumber"
    access: [read]
    min_session: "default"
    read_security_level:  0
    write_security_level: 1
    data_length: 17

dtcs:
  - code:        "0xC00100"
    description: "CAN bus communication loss"
    severity:    "check_at_next_halt"

routines:
  - id:   "0xFF00"
    name: "ECU_SelfTest"
    min_session:    "extended"
    security_level: 0
    support:        ["start", "results"]
```

DoIP transport extension (additive, optional — v1.6.0):

```yaml
ecu:
  transport: doip           # "can" (default) | "doip" | "both"
  doip:
    logical_address: "0xE400"   # This ECU's DoIP logical address
    source_address:  "0x0E00"   # Expected tester source address
    port:            13400       # Standard DoIP TCP port (ISO 13400)
```

Existing configs without an ecu: block continue to build unchanged (transport defaults to "can").

DID fields: id, name, access ([read] / [write] / [read, write]), min_session,
read_security_level (0=none), write_security_level (>=1 required for ASIL-B),
data_length, read_handler, write_handler, description

DTC fields: code (6-digit hex), description, severity
  severity values: "maintenance_only" | "check_at_next_halt" | "check_immediately"

## Code Generator CLI

python3 tools/codegen.py --config <yaml> --out <dir> [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --safety-wrappers       Generate ASIL-B 5-step DID safety wrappers (required for ASIL-B)
  --asil-level B          Target ASIL level
  --test-gen              Generate pytest test suite in <dir>/tests/
  --gui-types             Generate gui/src/generated/catalog.ts for the React dashboard
  --no-manifest           Skip manifest file (use in CI)

Generated files in <dir>:
  generated_config.h         Compile-time constants (CAN IDs, DID/DTC counts, timing)
  did_handlers.c/.h          DID read/write stubs — implement these in your application
  did_safety_wrappers.c/.h   ASIL-B 5-step validation chain (do not edit)
  routine_handlers.c/.h      Routine start/stop/results stubs
  uds_init.c/.h              Full UDS stack initialisation — EDS_DOIP_ONLY_BUILD guards included
  safety_config.h            Compile-time ASIL-B assertions
  tests/                     pytest suite (per-DID, per-routine, service protocol, firmware)

## ASIL-B Safety Chain

Every DID access passes 5 mandatory steps (generated, cannot be bypassed at runtime):
1. DID existence check        → NRC 0x31 (requestOutOfRange) if unknown
2. Session permission         → NRC 0x7F (serviceNotSupportedInActiveSession) if wrong
3. Security level check       → NRC 0x33 (securityAccessDenied) if insufficient
4. Access permission          → NRC 0x31 if read/write not allowed for this DID
5. Data length validation     → NRC 0x13 (incorrectMessageLength) if mismatch

## UDS Services

0x10 DiagnosticSessionControl  — default / programming / extended / safetySystem
0x11 ECUReset                  — hardReset / keyOffOnReset / softReset
0x14 ClearDiagnosticInformation
0x19 ReadDTCInformation        — sub-fns 0x01 0x02 0x04 0x06 0x0A
0x22 ReadDataByIdentifier      — multi-DID requests supported
0x27 SecurityAccess            — AES-128-CMAC, levels 1+2, TRNG-backed, NVM lockout
0x28 CommunicationControl
0x2E WriteDataByIdentifier     — ASIL-B safety chain enforced
0x31 RoutineControl            — Start / Stop / RequestResults
0x34 RequestDownload           — programming session + SecurityAccess required
0x35 RequestUpload             — ECU-to-tester data readback; read_cb required; programming session + SecurityAccess
0x36 TransferData              — CRC-32 accumulator; direction-aware (download or upload)
0x37 RequestTransferExit       — optional CRC-32 validation
0x3E TesterPresent             — S3Server timeout reset
0x85 ControlDTCSetting         — DTCSettingOn / DTCSettingOff

Not implemented (return NRC 0x11): 0x23 0x24 0x29 0x2A 0x2C 0x38 0x3D 0x86 0x87

## Build Commands

# Zephyr native_sim (CAN/ISO-TP, no hardware):
west build -b native_sim examples/basic_ecu \
  -- -DDTC_OVERLAY_FILE=boards/native_sim/native_sim.overlay

# Zephyr native_sim (DoIP, no hardware):
west build -b native_sim examples/basic_ecu_doip \
  -- -DEDS_DOIP_ONLY_BUILD \
     -DEXTRA_CONF_FILE=boards/native_sim/native_sim_doip.conf

# FreeRTOS QEMU Cortex-M4:
cmake -B build -DEDS_PLATFORM=freertos -DFREERTOS_DIR=/opt/freertos-kernel \
  -GNinja examples/basic_ecu_freertos && ninja -C build

# Run unit tests (37 modules, includes 24 DoIP tests):
bash build_tests.sh

# Run harness tests (68 tests):
bash build_harness.sh --run

# Run generated pytest suite (simulator mode):
cd examples/basic_ecu/generated/tests && pytest . --can-interface=simulator -v

# Run DoIP integration tests (requires xaloqi-tester installed):
pytest tests/test_doip_integration.py -v --timeout=60

## Repository Structure

core/               UDS server, session FSM, security, safety chain, 16 service handlers
transport/          ISO-TP state machine, CAN transport abstraction
  doip/             DoIP ECU server (ISO 13400-2) — Zephyr and FreeRTOS+LwIP bindings (v1.6.0)
config/             DID database, DTC database, NVM DTC mirror
platform/
  platform_api.h    Common HAL interface (Zephyr + FreeRTOS)
  zephyr/           Zephyr RTOS port (threads, CAN, NVM, flash, watchdog, DoIP shim)
  freertos/         FreeRTOS port (task, CAN shim, NVM abstraction, DoIP shim)
tools/              codegen.py, testgen.py, mcp_server.py, 14 Jinja2 templates
examples/           11 ECU examples — each with diagnostics_config.yaml + generated/
gui/                React/TypeScript configurator and live dashboard
tests/              37 Unity unit tests, Python integration tests, harness (68 tests)
ide/vscode-extension/  YAML validation, hover docs, Run Codegen command

## Requirements

Zephyr RTOS v3.7+, West 1.2+, CMake >= 3.20, Python 3.9+
FreeRTOS-Kernel (any recent release, tested with HEAD)
arm-none-eabi-gcc for FreeRTOS / hardware Zephyr targets
LwIP 2.x for FreeRTOS DoIP targets
Node.js 20 LTS for GUI

## NRC Quick Reference

0x11 serviceNotSupported       — SID not in dispatch table
0x12 subFunctionNotSupported   — unknown sub-function
0x13 incorrectMessageLength    — DID data length mismatch (safety step 5)
0x22 conditionsNotCorrect      — access permission denied
0x24 requestSequenceError      — SecurityAccess: SendKey without prior RequestSeed
0x31 requestOutOfRange         — DID unknown or write not permitted
0x33 securityAccessDenied      — required security level not active (safety step 3)
0x35 invalidKey                — wrong SecurityAccess key
0x36 exceededNumberOfAttempts  — too many failed SecurityAccess attempts
0x37 requiredTimeDelayNotExpired — SecurityAccess lockout timer running
0x7F serviceNotSupportedInActiveSession — session not allowed (safety step 2)

## For AI Assistants

Full YAML schema, codegen CLI reference, all DID patterns, DoIP integration, FreeRTOS
integration, safety architecture detail, and troubleshooting: see docs/AI_CONTEXT.md
